bead.title: routing P1: ddx applies shared failure-classification table; single-owner one-hop middle->max escalation
bead.description: Implement the ddx side of the FEAT-004 failure-classification table: ddx work maps each attempt outcome to exactly the action in the addendum table (success/already_satisfied=close; no_changes=close/unclaim NO tier-escalation; genuine impl/capability failure=escalate middle->max ONCE; dirty=clean+same-tier retry; transport=same-tier reroute; quota-exhausted=global pool cooldown; auth/setup=operator-attention; timeout=clean+retry then operator-attention; merge-conflict=unclaim+retry; max genuine failure=operator-attention). ddx owns the ONE middle->max tier escalation (per the escalation-owner decision bead fizeau-994604ec); a per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap prevents multi-worker stampede. Depends on the escalation-owner contract + the shared classifier (fizeau-44e98499).
bead.acceptance: 1. ddx classifies each outcome per the addendum table (table-driven test). 2. Exactly one middle->max escalation, only on typed genuine failure; no_changes/dirty/transport/quota/auth do NOT escalate. 3. Per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap enforced (test simulates concurrent genuine failures, asserts cap). 4. quota-exhausted -> global pool cooldown, not N per-bead mutations (test). 5. cd /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green. 6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
